safest_plan.entry_basis: Zero live 2025-05-28: green demand/order-block band marked at the 1.272 fib ~218–222 ('Level has been ignored the first time, now acted as a BB'); the extracted text framed it as a 'pullback to 215–220'. Note price on the chart (206) is already BELOW this zone, so it is a reclaim, not a buy from current price.

targets.basis: Zero live chart label '225$ Target?' and extracted text TP1 (225 previous key swing)
Zero live chart 'HTF SUPPLY 240$' band; extracted text 240–250 higher-timeframe supply area

safest_plan.stop_basis: below the 194 break-of-structure swing (chart 'BOS' / '194$' label, 0.62 fib); a daily close under 194 voids the long thesis

stance_by_source.summary: One live-session chart marking a bullish continuation: reclaim the 218–222 demand zone, aim for 225 then the 240 supply, with the trade dead on a close below 194.

conflicts: The chart's marked buy zone (about 218–222) is ABOVE the chart's last price of 206, yet the extracted note calls it a 'pullback' entry — so the bullish plan needs price to first climb back up and reclaim ~211–218, it is not a dip-buy from here.
The chart shows two deeper support zones the text leaves out — a band near 190 and a weekly buy zone around 167–175 — which are the real downside levels if the setup fails.
Only one source exists and it is dated 2025-05-28, roughly 13 months old, so the levels may already be stale; nothing fresh confirms the setup still holds.
Reward to the first target (218 up to 225, about 7 points) is small versus the risk down to the 194 stop (about 24 points), well under a 2-to-1 payoff.
plain_english: Only one read exists for Amazon, from a Zero teaching session over a year ago, and it is bullish: it wants to buy when price returns into a support shelf around 218–222 and then ride toward 225 and 240, calling the trade off if the daily candle closes below 194. The catch is that the chart's last price (206) is already below that buy shelf, so this is a wait-and-reclaim idea, not a buy-now — and the small reward versus the deeper risk makes it weak. Because it is a single, stale source with a poor risk-to-reward, stay on the sidelines and wait for a fresh, confirmed move back above the 211–218 area before considering anything.
